metadata.title: Crypto market-makers & political money — Jump/Terra, the Jane Street pedigree, the liquidity layer, and the Fairshake super-PAC machine
metadata.status: trades, settlements, PAC totals, and personnel pedigrees are FACT (court filings / SEC / FEC / public record); the 'market-makers are the hidden load-bearing layer' and 'crypto bought the rules' reads are graded interpretation
metadata.frame: Two under-drawn connective layers. (1) MARKET-MAKERS — the high-frequency trading firms that provide crypto's liquidity are also where the collapse cluster came FROM (Jump propped Terra's peg; SBF and Ellison are Jane Street alumni) and where systemic opacity concentrates. (2) POLITICAL MONEY — the same firms fund Fairshake, the largest super-PAC of the 2024/2026 cycles (~$193M), which bridges the crypto industry directly to Congress and the legislation it wanted (GENIUS Act; cf. spec-crypto-legislation-forcing). Together they connect spec-crypto-collapse-cluster, spec-crypto-enforcement-actors, and influence-operator-network.

the_jump_terra_node: Jump Crypto (the digital-asset arm of HFT giant Jump Trading; Kanav Kariya ran it) secretly PROPPED the TerraUSD peg in May 2021 — buying UST to defend the $1 peg in exchange for deeply-discounted LUNA — booking an alleged ~$1.28B profit, then exiting before the May-2022 collapse. The SEC charged Jump's subsidiary Tai Mo Shan ~$123M (disgorgement + interest + penalty) for misleading investors about UST's stability; the CFTC opened a separate probe; and Terraform's bankruptcy administrator sued Jump for ~$4B. The point: the 'algorithmic' stablecoin was manually propped by a market-maker, and its 'organic' peg was a managed illusion — verify the claim. grade: fact (the SEC settlement, the trade, the suits).
the_jane_street_pedigree: The FTX/Alameda principals came from elite quant trading: Sam Bankman-Fried and Caroline Ellison both worked at Jane Street before Alameda. The pedigree that lent FTX credibility was the same quant-arbitrage culture — a reminder that elite-firm provenance is not integrity (the Mysten/FTX-Ventures point generalized). grade: fact (the employment history).
the_liquidity_layer: The crypto market-making oligopoly — Jump, Wintermute, Cumberland (DRW), GSR, B2C2, and TradFi entrants like Citadel Securities (see spec-blockchain-ecosystem) and Jane Street — provides most on/off-exchange liquidity. It is largely OPAQUE (private firms, OTC desks, cross-venue books), concentrated, and conflicted (often investors AND market-makers AND token-launch partners in the same assets). When a few MMs make the market, 'price discovery' is thinner and more manipulable than the screens suggest — the systemic-opacity analogue to the Aladdin monoculture (spec-blackrock-aladdin-concentration). grade: the firms + roles FACT; the opacity/concentration read is interpretation.
the_political_machine: Fairshake is the crypto industry's super-PAC — one of the best-funded in the country, with ~$193M marshaled for the 2026 cycle (after ~$130M+ deployed in 2024 that helped defeat crypto-skeptics like Sherrod Brown). Backers: Coinbase (>$75M in 2024 + $25M committed for 2026), a16z (~$23M), Ripple, Jump Crypto, Uniswap Labs, Gemini. The machine bridges the industry directly to Congress and produced the legislative wins (GENIUS Act; market-structure bills) the enforcement-era firms wanted — i.e. after losing in court and at the SEC, the industry bought the RULES at the ballot box. grade: PAC totals + donors FACT (FEC); 'bought the rules' is labeled interpretation, not an allegation of illegality (super-PAC spending is legal).
the_honest_reading: The market-makers are the hidden load-bearing layer of crypto — they ARE the liquidity, they SEEDED the collapse (Jump/Terra), they STAFFED the fraud (Jane Street -> Alameda), and via Fairshake they now WRITE the political environment that governs all of it. None of this is per se illegal (the Jump UST settlement aside); the finding is structural concentration + conflict-of-role + the conversion of market power into political power. Intent is not imputed beyond the adjudicated SEC settlement. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

edges.note: OVERLAY: the 'algorithmic' peg was manually propped
